title: IRGC Commander Threatens 'Long and Painful Strikes' on US Regional Positions If Attacks Resume
summary: A senior IRGC official warned that any renewed US military strikes on Iran would provoke sustained retaliatory attacks on American positions across the Gulf region, as Tehran activated air defenses in anticipation of potential action.
contents:
## Iran Escalates Rhetoric as Ceasefire Tensions Mount

### IRGC Threat Assessment
A senior official of Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps declared that any new US attack, even a limited one, would trigger "long and painful strikes" on American regional positions. IRGC Aerospace Force Commander Majid Mousavi amplified the threat, stating: "We've seen what happened to your regional bases, we will see the same thing happen to your warships."

### Air Defense Activation
Late Thursday, air defense activity occurred in parts of Tehran, with Iran's semi-official Mehr news agency reporting engagement of "small drones and unmanned surveillance aerial vehicles." Iranian military sources indicated Tehran had assessed the likelihood of a "short, intensive US strike, possibly followed by an Israeli attack," and prepared accordingly.

### Supreme Leader's Statement
Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei issued a written message declaring that "the enemies' abuses of the waterway" would face elimination under new management of the strait, signaling Tehran's intent to maintain control over the Strait of Hormuz. He stated: "Foreigners who come from thousands of kilometres away have no place there except at the bottom of its waters."

### Gulf State Reactions
The UAE banned citizens from traveling to Iran, Lebanon, and Iraq. UAE presidential adviser Anwar Gargash rejected Iranian assurances, citing "treacherous aggression against all its neighbours." Bahrain's King Hamad condemned Iranian aggression and threatened internal collaborators with imprisonment and citizenship revocation.

### Sources
Al Jazeera, IRNA, Mehr News Agency, Reuters.
